title: Same Day Wire Release Control
description: A payments-and-settlement workflow that controls release, routing, and execution timing across multiple rails and liquidity constraints. Focus case: same day wire release control.
assumptions: Central bank or rail finality semantics hold as modeled; once released, finality is achieved per rail rules.
Resource pools (liquidity, manifests, accounts) are represented atomically and reservations are exclusive.
Operator approvals and external rail acceptances occur within same‑day time horizons required by workflow.
Mapping tables between participants and rails are available and consistent for routing/rail binding.
risks: Insufficient intraday liquidity causing failed reservations and subsequent race conditions.
Conflicting routing choices across rails leading to duplicate or divergent execution attempts.
Operator approval delays or missing manual confirmations delaying final release.
Compliance or validation failures after resource reservation causing partial state and stranded locks.
Inter‑rail timing mismatches where a payment is released on one rail but fails on another, producing settlement mismatch.
